message:
fix: node_degree_xy iter order matches nx + self-loop edges(nbunch) (br-r37-c1-6yimw)

Two related fixes in this commit:

1. node_degree_xy: nx iterates xdeg(set(nodes)) which yields nodes
   in hash-set order (since the implementation calls set(G) and
   iterates that). The previous fnx loop walked present_nodes in
   G.nodes()-insertion order, producing the same multiset of
   (degree_x, degree_y) pairs but in a different sequence.

   Repro:
     edges = [(c,d),(a,b),(b,c),(d,e),(a,c)]
     fnx (pre-fix) -> [(3,2),(3,2),(3,2),(2,3),(2,1),(2,2),(2,3),(2,2),(2,3),(1,2)]
     nx            -> [(2,2),(2,3),(2,3),(2,1),(2,2),(2,3),(1,2),(3,2),(3,2),(3,2)]

   Replicate nx's exact algorithm: iterate xdeg(set(nodes)) in
   hash order, then for each u stream G.edges(u) → ydeg(neighbors).

2. edges(nbunch) self-loop regression: my earlier br-r37-c1-dc14n fix
   moved the ``seen.add(node)`` to BEFORE the adj loop, which ate
   self-loop edges (nbr==node was already in ``seen`` when the inner
   loop reached it). nx adds to ``seen`` AFTER the adj loop so
   self-loops still emit once. The fix moves the ``seen.add`` to
   the post-loop position. Verified: G([(0,0),(0,1)]).edges(0) now
   yields [(0,0),(0,1)] matching nx (was [(0,1)] only).

13 parity tests in test_node_degree_xy_iter_order_parity.py freeze
the contract: str-node repro + path + complete + DiGraph
out/in + DiGraph in/out + nodes= + weight= + multigraph multiset
+ multigraph iter-order + empty + isolated-nodes + self-loops +
multiset preservation. The prior 12 edges(nbunch) tests in
test_edges_nbunch_order_parity.py still pass (the post-loop
seen.add doesn't change non-self-loop behaviour).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -27,6 +27,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-6qcaw","title":"center / periphery node iteration order drifts from nx","description":"fnx.center and fnx.periphery use the Rust _raw_* implementations which return nodes in canonical / Rust-internal order rather than nx's node-insertion order.

Repro: balanced_tree(2, 3)
  fnx.periphery -> [10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 7, 8, 9]
  nx.periphery  -> [7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13, 14]

Eccentricity values match between the two libs; only the periphery's iteration order differs. nx's contract is to iterate the eccentricity dict (which has node-insertion-order keys) and emit nodes whose eccentricity equals the max (periphery) or min (center).

Drop-in code that compared the periphery/center list to a reference broke.

Fix: replicate nx's pattern explicitly — iterate G.nodes() in node-insertion order and filter by max/min eccentricity. Mirrors the cycle_basis / find_cliques / SCC delegation pattern.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T17:12:46.734769584Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T17:16:18.802671820Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T17:16:18.800244863Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-6rphu","title":"parity: 5 all_pairs_dijkstra/bellman_ford functions have wrong inner-dict iteration order","description":"Continuation of br-r37-c1-3dxfn (outer keys) and br-r37-c1-5ur50 (BFS inner). Inner per-source dicts of all_pairs_dijkstra_path, all_pairs_dijkstra_path_length, all_pairs_bellman_ford_path, all_pairs_bellman_ford_path_length, and all_pairs_dijkstra (tuple form) iterate in arbitrary Rust internal order. nx returns inner dicts in (distance, BFS-tiebreak) order from each source. Drop-in code that does 'for src in result: for tgt in result[src].items(): ...' breaks. Fix: reorder each inner dict with _reorder_by_distance(dists, G=G, source=source).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:33:39.557444500Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:36:49.867369075Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:36:49.865369156Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 5cab4dc. 5 all_pairs dijkstra/bellman_ford functions now yield inner dicts in distance-order matching nx. 9 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-6sgls","title":"MultiGraph difference / symmetric_difference drop parallel edges vs nx","description":"fnx.difference and fnx.symmetric_difference on MultiGraphs collapse parallel edges to a single edge — losing edges that should be in the result. The simple-graph case was already fixed (intersection: br-r37-c1-saf8a, others verified) but the MultiGraph variants drift.

Repro:
  mg1 = MultiGraph with edges (a,b,0), (a,b,1), (b,c,0)
  mg2 = MultiGraph with edges (a,b,0), (c,d,0)
  
  fnx.difference(mg1, mg2).edges(keys=True) -> [('b','c',0)]
  nx.difference(mgx1, mgx2).edges(keys=True)  -> [('a','b',1), ('b','c',0)]
  
  fnx.symmetric_difference(mg1, mg2).edges(keys=True) -> [('b','c',0), ('c','d',0)]
  nx.symmetric_difference                              -> [('a','b',1), ('b','c',0), ('c','d',0)]

The fnx Rust path treats MultiGraph edges as simple-graph (drops parallels). intersection works correctly because parallel edges that exist in both graphs are kept.

Fix: delegate MultiGraph difference / symmetric_difference to nx (mirrors br-r37-c1-saf8a's intersection fix). Simple-graph case unchanged.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T18:05:45.205592563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T18:08:36.330917751Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T18:08:36.328853453Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-6yimw","title":"node_degree_xy iteration order drifts from nx","description":"fnx.node_degree_xy yields tuples in G.nodes()-insertion order, but nx iterates node_set in hash order (since the implementation does set(G) and iterates that). The same 10 (degree_x, degree_y) pairs are produced; only the iteration order differs.

Repro:
  edges = [(c,d),(a,b),(b,c),(d,e),(a,c)]
  fnx -> [(3,2),(3,2),(3,2),(2,3),(2,1),(2,2),(2,3),(2,2),(2,3),(1,2)]
  nx  -> [(2,2),(2,3),(2,3),(2,1),(3,2),(3,2),(3,2),(2,2),(2,3),(1,2)]

  Sorted: both produce identical multisets of pairs.

Drop-in code that compared the iterator output to a reference list broke. Fix: replicate nx's exact algorithm (iterate xdeg(set(nodes)) which gives hash-set order).","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T19:13:04.535552901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T19:14:00.432809240Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-71glx","title":"parity: 3 signature mismatches (gaussian_random_partition_graph arg order, find_minimal_d_separator wrong sig, to_nested_tuple leaks _parent)","description":"Three signature-parity defects discovered while signature-diffing fnx __all__ vs upstream nx (excluding backend dispatch noise). (1) gaussian_random_partition_graph: nx is (n, s, v, p_in, p_out, directed=False, seed=None) but fnx is (n, s, v, p_in, p_out, seed=None, directed=False) — positional arg order swapped, breaks calls like nx.gaussian_random_partition_graph(10, 5, 0.1, 0.4, 0.05, True). (2) find_minimal_d_separator: nx is (G, x, y, *, included=None, restricted=None) but fnx is (G, u, v) — wrong param names AND missing keyword-only included/restricted. (3) to_nested_tuple: fnx leaks an internal _parent=None recursion arg in its public signature. Fix: align signatures; for find_minimal_d_separator, route through nx via _call_networkx_for_parity to handle the new kwargs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T00:49:37.606667646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T01:04:38.730801947Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T01:04:38.728883324Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 21b0340: 3 signature fixes (gaussian_random_partition_graph arg order, find_minimal_d_separator delegated to nx with included/restricted kwargs, to_nested_tuple no longer leaks _parent). Smoke test still passes 170/170.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-75r94","title":"parity: coloring strategy_* (8) + matching_dict_to_set (1) wrappers","description":"Two clusters of nx utilities not surfaced. Coloring strategies (used as strategy= argument to greedy_color): strategy_largest_first(G, colors), strategy_random_sequential(G, colors, seed=None), strategy_smallest_last(G, colors), strategy_independent_set(G, colors), strategy_connected_sequential_bfs(G, colors), strategy_connected_sequential_dfs(G, colors), strategy_connected_sequential(G, colors, traversal=bfs), strategy_saturation_largest_first(G, colors). Matching utility: matching_dict_to_set(matching) — converts a {node: partner} dict to a set of (u, v) frozensets. Add Python wrappers in __init__.py exporting at top-level + __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T23:29:37.916763877Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T23:44:39.656055329Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T23:44:39.653993535Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 7939284: 8 strategy_* coloring callables + matching_dict_to_set. Verified with nx.greedy_color(C6, strategy=fnx.strategy_largest_first) matching nx output. Follow-up: fnx.greedy_color does not accept callable strategies (only strings) — separate parity gap.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-7u6up","title":"parity: community edge_betweenness_partition + greedy_source_expansion missing","description":"Two community-detection helpers from networkx.algorithms.community not exposed by franken_networkx: edge_betweenness_partition(G, number_of_sets, weight=None) and greedy_source_expansion(G, source, ...). Add Python wrappers delegating via _call_networkx_community_for_parity, export at top-level and add to __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T12:13:47.981273951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T13:05:50.852667948Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T13:05:50.850778351Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 4eb0d6d: edge_betweenness_partition + greedy_source_expansion verified on karate","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

                 else:

                     result.append((node, nbr, edge_data.get(data, default)))

@@ -20850,47 +20854,28 @@ def node_attribute_xy(G, attribute, nodes=None):

-    def directed_degree_map(mode):

-            total = 0

-                        total += attrs.get(weight, 1) if weight is not None else 1
-                else:

+    br-r37-c1-6yimw: nx iterates ``xdeg(set(nodes))`` which yields
+    nodes in hash-set order (since the implementation calls
+    ``set(G)`` and iterates that). The previous fnx loop walked
+    ``present_nodes`` (G.nodes()-insertion order), drifting tuple
+    iteration order from nx. Replicate nx's exact algorithm.

+    node_set = set(G) if nodes is None else set(nodes)
     if G.is_directed():

-        ydeg = directed_degree_map(y)

-                    yield (xdeg[u], ydeg[v])

@@ -0,0 +1,161 @@
+"""Parity for ``node_degree_xy`` iterator order.
+
+Bead br-r37-c1-6yimw. nx iterates ``xdeg(set(nodes))`` which yields
+nodes in hash-set order (since the implementation calls
+``set(G)`` and iterates that). The previous fnx loop walked
+``present_nodes`` in G.nodes()-insertion order, producing the same
+multiset of (degree_x, degree_y) pairs but in a different sequence.
+
+Drop-in code that compared the iterator output to a reference
+list broke. Fix replicates nx's exact algorithm.
+
+Repro:
+  edges = [(c,d),(a,b),(b,c),(d,e),(a,c)]
+  fnx (pre-fix) -> [(3,2),(3,2),(3,2),(2,3),(2,1),(2,2),(2,3),(2,2),(2,3),(1,2)]
+  nx            -> [(2,2),(2,3),(2,3),(2,1),(2,2),(2,3),(1,2),(3,2),(3,2),(3,2)]
+  (Sorted: identical.)
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import pytest
+
+import franken_networkx as fnx
+
+try:
+    import networkx as nx
+    HAS_NX = True
+except ImportError:
+    HAS_NX = False
+
+needs_nx = pytest.mark.skipif(not HAS_NX, reason="networkx not installed")
+
+
+def _make_graph(lib, edges):
+    g = lib.Graph()
+    for u, v in edges:
+        g.add_edge(u, v)
+    return g
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_repro_str_node_iter_matches_nx():
+    edges = [("c", "d"), ("a", "b"), ("b", "c"), ("d", "e"), ("a", "c")]
+    g = _make_graph(fnx, edges)
+    gx = _make_graph(nx, edges)
+    assert list(fnx.node_degree_xy(g)) == list(nx.node_degree_xy(gx))
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_int_node_path_matches_nx():
+    g = fnx.path_graph(5)
+    gx = nx.path_graph(5)
+    assert list(fnx.node_degree_xy(g)) == list(nx.node_degree_xy(gx))
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_complete_graph_matches_nx():
+    g = fnx.complete_graph(4)
+    gx = nx.complete_graph(4)
+    assert list(fnx.node_degree_xy(g)) == list(nx.node_degree_xy(gx))
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_directed_graph_x_out_y_in_matches_nx():
+    edges = [("a", "b"), ("b", "c"), ("c", "a"), ("a", "d")]
+    dg = fnx.DiGraph()
+    dgx = nx.DiGraph()
+    for u, v in edges:
+        dg.add_edge(u, v)
+        dgx.add_edge(u, v)
+    assert list(fnx.node_degree_xy(dg, x="out", y="in")) == list(
+        nx.node_degree_xy(dgx, x="out", y="in")
+    )
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_directed_graph_x_in_y_out_matches_nx():
+    edges = [("a", "b"), ("b", "c"), ("c", "a"), ("a", "d")]
+    dg = fnx.DiGraph()
+    dgx = nx.DiGraph()
+    for u, v in edges:
+        dg.add_edge(u, v)
+        dgx.add_edge(u, v)
+    assert list(fnx.node_degree_xy(dg, x="in", y="out")) == list(
+        nx.node_degree_xy(dgx, x="in", y="out")
+    )
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_with_nodes_kwarg_matches_nx():
+    edges = [("c", "d"), ("a", "b"), ("b", "c"), ("d", "e"), ("a", "c")]
+    g = _make_graph(fnx, edges)
+    gx = _make_graph(nx, edges)
+    assert list(fnx.node_degree_xy(g, nodes=["a", "b", "c"])) == list(
+        nx.node_degree_xy(gx, nodes=["a", "b", "c"])
+    )
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_with_weight_matches_nx():
+    g = fnx.Graph()
+    gx = nx.Graph()
+    for u, v, w in [("a", "b", 1), ("b", "c", 2), ("c", "a", 3)]:
+        g.add_edge(u, v, weight=w)
+        gx.add_edge(u, v, weight=w)
+    assert list(fnx.node_degree_xy(g, weight="weight")) == list(
+        nx.node_degree_xy(gx, weight="weight")
+    )
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_multigraph_yields_per_parallel_edge():
+    """MultiGraph: each (u, v, key) parallel edge should produce
+    its own (degree, degree) tuple."""
+    mg = fnx.MultiGraph([("a", "b"), ("a", "b"), ("b", "c"), ("c", "a")])
+    mgx = nx.MultiGraph([("a", "b"), ("a", "b"), ("b", "c"), ("c", "a")])
+    f = list(fnx.node_degree_xy(mg))
+    n = list(nx.node_degree_xy(mgx))
+    # Sorted match (parallel edges produce identical tuples that
+    # appear multiple times) — verify count and multiset.
+    assert sorted(f) == sorted(n)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_multigraph_iter_order_matches_nx():
+    mg = fnx.MultiGraph([("a", "b"), ("a", "b"), ("b", "c"), ("c", "a")])
+    mgx = nx.MultiGraph([("a", "b"), ("a", "b"), ("b", "c"), ("c", "a")])
+    assert list(fnx.node_degree_xy(mg)) == list(nx.node_degree_xy(mgx))
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_empty_graph_yields_empty():
+    g = fnx.Graph()
+    gx = nx.Graph()
+    assert list(fnx.node_degree_xy(g)) == list(nx.node_degree_xy(gx)) == []
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_isolated_nodes_no_edges_no_tuples():
+    g = fnx.Graph()
+    g.add_nodes_from([0, 1, 2])
+    gx = nx.Graph()
+    gx.add_nodes_from([0, 1, 2])
+    assert list(fnx.node_degree_xy(g)) == list(nx.node_degree_xy(gx)) == []
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_self_loops_treated_as_one_edge():
+    g = fnx.Graph([(0, 0), (0, 1)])
+    gx = nx.Graph([(0, 0), (0, 1)])
+    assert list(fnx.node_degree_xy(g)) == list(nx.node_degree_xy(gx))
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_multiset_of_tuples_matches_nx():
+    """Even before the fix, the multiset of tuples matched —
+    sanity-check that property is preserved."""
+    edges = [("c", "d"), ("a", "b"), ("b", "c"), ("d", "e"), ("a", "c")]
+    g = _make_graph(fnx, edges)
+    gx = _make_graph(nx, edges)
+    assert sorted(fnx.node_degree_xy(g)) == sorted(nx.node_degree_xy(gx))
